channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLscienceAndDiscovery, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Scientists resurrect 3.2-billion-year-old enzyme to reveal how life began on Earth
item.description: Researchers rebuilt ancient versions of a key enzyme that makes nitrogen available to life, providing a rare look into Earth's early biology.

item.title: Schrödinger’s anthill: Quantum entanglement found in a crystal large enough to hold
item.description: A centimeter-sized crystal has shown clear signs of quantum entanglement, suggesting that large objects can exhibit deep quantum behavior.

item.title: Ancient DNA reveals the mysterious collapse of Europe's megalith builders
item.description: DNA from a 5,000-year-old French megalithic tomb shows that individuals buried before and after a population collapse were genetically unrelated.

item.title: Incredible new material makes heat programmable
item.description: A newly developed material can control and "program" heat, directing thermal radiation, switching modes, and retaining settings without continuous power.
